State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • •    Administration • •    Executive/Legal • •    Enforcement • •    Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          327-5660               322-5901            322-6441               322-6444

February 15, 1983

Robert H. Burnham
Acting City Attorney
City of Newport Beach
City Hall
3300 Newport Blvd.
Newport Beach, CA  92263

Re:  Our Advice No. A-83-016

Dear Mr. Burnham:

     Thank you for your letter of January 18, 1983, requesting
advice on the interpretation of Government Code Section 84308.
You asked whether the city council, when acting in a quasi-
judicial capacity, is exempt from this Section.

     Government Code Section 84308(d) exempts from the Section's
restrictions "legislative bodies such as city councils, county
boards of supervisors, and the State Legislature, while acting
as a body or a committee thereof."  This exemption applies to
all actions of a legislative body, such as a city council,
regardless of whether the actions are legislative or quasi-
judicial, so long as the council acts "as a body or
committee thereof."*

     Therefore, although the city council may act in a quasi-
judicial role, it is still exempt from the provisions of
Government Code Section 84308.

     If you have any further questions, please let us know.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Susan M. Harrigan
                                        Administrative Assistant

SMH:km

*For a definition of when a city council "acts as a body or
a committee thereof, see 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.4.

CITY OF NEWPORT BEACH
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
(714) 640-2201

January 18, 1983

State of California
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1100 K Street Building
P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804

Attention: Legal Division

Re: Government Code Section 84308/Recent FPPC Regulations

This office is in receipt of recent regulation adopted by the
Fair Political Practices Commission which implement provisions of
new legislation regarding campaign contributions solicited by or
made to, members of certain quasi-judicial boards.

After reviewing the legislation, and your regulations, this
office is uncertain as to the status of the City Council of the
City of Newport Beach in the following context. The City Council
of Newport Beach is primarily a legislative body but quite
frequently hears appeals from denials, by staff or Planning
Commission, of various licenses or permits. Would you please
advise us if the City Council, when acting in such a quasi-
judicial fashion, is still exempt from the provisions of the
legislation referenced above.

Sincerely,

ROBERT H. BURNHAM
Acting City Attorney

RHB/bf

City Hall • 3300 Newport Boulevard, Newport Beach, California 92663
